2605.10893.title: Grounded or Guessing? LVLM Confidence Estimation via Blind-Image Contrastive Ranking
2605.10893.paper_summary: Large vision-language models suffer from visual ungroundedness: they can produce a fluent, confident, and even correct response driven entirely by language priors, with the image contributing nothing to the prediction. Existing confidence estimation methods cannot detect this, as they observe model behavior under normal inference with no mechanism to determine whether a prediction was shaped by the image or by text alone. We introduce BICR (Blind-Image Contrastive Ranking), a model-agnostic confidence estimation framework that makes this contrast explicit during training by extracting hidden states from a frozen LVLM twice: once with the real image-question pair, and once with the image blacked out while the question is held fixed. A lightweight probe is trained on the real-image hidden state and regularized by a ranking loss that penalizes higher confidence on the blacked-out view, teaching it to treat visual grounding as a signal of reliability at zero additional inference cost. Evaluated across five modern LVLMs and seven baselines on a benchmark covering visual question answering, object hallucination detection, medical imaging, and financial document understanding, BICR achieves the best cross-LVLM average on both calibration and discrimination simultaneously, with statistically significant discrimination gains robust to cluster-aware analysis at 4-18x fewer parameters than the strongest probing baseline.
2605.10893.paper_summary_zh: 大型視覺-語言模型面臨視覺無根據的問題：它們可以產生流暢、自信，甚至正確的回應，這完全是由語言先驗驅動，圖像對預測沒有任何貢獻。現有的信心估計方法無法檢測到這一點，因為它們在正常推理下觀察模型行為，沒有機制來確定預測是由圖像還是僅由文本塑造的。我們引入了BICR（盲圖對比排名），這是一個模型無關的信心估計框架，在訓練過程中通過從凍結的LVLM中提取隱藏狀態來明確這一對比：一次是使用真實的圖像-問題對，另一次是在問題固定的情況下將圖像遮蔽。輕量級探針在真實圖像的隱藏狀態上進行訓練，並通過一個排名損失進行正則化，該損失懲罰在遮蔽視圖上的高信心，教導它將視覺根據視為可靠性的信號，而不增加額外的推理成本。在涵蓋視覺問題回答、物體幻覺檢測、醫學影像和金融文件理解的基準上，BICR在五個現代LVLM和七個基準上進行評估，實現了在校準和區分上同時達到最佳的跨LVLM平均值，並且在參數數量比最強探針基準少4-18倍的情況下，獲得了統計上顯著的區分增益，對集群感知分析具有穩健性。
2605.10893.author: Reza Khanmohammadi et.al.
2605.10893.authors: Reza Khanmohammadi, Erfan Miahi, Simerjot Kaur, Charese H. Smiley, Ivan Brugere, Kundan Thind, Mohammad M. Ghassemi
